supersedes: Run 4 (same day, post-SF2 evening snapshot). Run 3's exact findings.md/snapshot.json are preserved in git at commit 399ec52 and Run 4's at commit f5a8623; findings.md in this folder keeps the full Run 3 + Run 4 text with the Run 5 section appended.

ESP.optaNote: DERIVED, not printed by Opta: Opta's post-SF1 refresh (TNT Sports, Jul 14-15, pre-SF2) published ESP 56.15 / ENG 23.38 / ARG 20.47 (sums to 100.00) with ENG 52.53% to beat ARG. Conditioning on the known SF2 result gives ARG 20.47/47.47 = 43.1% and ESP 56.9%. Back-solves exactly (23.38/52.53 => ESP beats ENG 55.49%; 55.49*0.5253 + 56.88*0.4747 = 56.15). Run 4's stale-low 0.234 is retired.

ARG.optaNote: same derivation; with one match left, title odds = final-match odds

ENG.optaNote: resolved to 0 by elimination Jul 15 (lost SF2 1-2 to Argentina); Opta's pre-SF2 refresh had ENG at 23.38%

optaPublishedPostSF1.source: TNT Sports (region-blocked from this vantage; figures extracted via multiple independent search-result snippets, trusted because they sum to 100.00 and back-solve exactly)

market.note: Re-checked late Jul 15: no material movement since Run 4. Overround-normalized, every book lands ~59/41.
market.correction: A first-pass search summary attributed '-136' to BetMGM, implying a sharp move to Argentina. Fetching the source showed -136 is KALSHI, already in Run 4's snapshot. No move occurred.

anchorConvergence.note: First pass where all anchors are fresh simultaneously. Four independent anchors inside a five-point spread, all inside the unchanged author range.

sf2_ENG_v_ARG.source: Opta match preview Jul 15; 90-min split ENG 37.3 / draw 30.7 / ARG 32.0. The post-SF1 bracket refresh carried a slightly different 52.53% for the same call - both real, different artifacts.

outcomes.note: No match played since Run 4. Final: Spain v Argentina, Jul 19, MetLife, 3 p.m. ET. Grading of Run 1-4 scorecards belongs to the score step.

carriedForwardFromRun4: All Run 1-4 verified facts stand: Spain 2-0 France with the disputed Digne-on-Yamal penalty; England 1-2 Argentina (Gordon 55'; E. Fernandez 85', L. Martinez 90+2' from a Messi cross; ARG 64% possession, xG ~1.84 v ~0.53; referee Elfath, no controversy of substance); market flip to Spain; Silver paywall note; penalty superlative softening; five named banks; Miami Herald ~$90M; La Nacion $260M/$57M attribution; Tofoni ~3h videoconference interview (witness, not charged); Tapia travel-authorization nugget; AFA pushback quote; no US charges; FBI declined comment; no suspensions for the final.
newSinceRun4: RESOLVED Run 4's deferred item: Opta HAD refreshed post-SF1 - the main semi-final article never moved, but the bracket re-ran and TNT Sports published the set (ESP 56.15 / ENG 23.38 / ARG 20.47, sums to 100.00; ENG 52.53% to beat ARG). Run 4 checked the article and reasonably concluded 'not refreshed'; the refresh lived elsewhere.

Because that vintage predates SF2, the published ARG 20.47% still priced a 47.47% chance of merely reaching the final. models.ts now carries Opta's numbers CONDITIONED on the known result: ESP .569 / ARG .431 (20.47/47.47 = 43.1%). Verified by exact back-solve. Disclosed as a derivation in models.ts, whowins-intro prose, and the source 'why' - never presented as a figure Opta printed.

First pass where every anchor is fresh at once, and they agree: Opta ~57/43, books ~59/41, Kalshi 57.7/42.4, Elo bracket ~56/44 - all inside the unchanged author range 55-60 / 40-45. Corroboration, so AUTHOR_RANGES held rather than nudged.

Market re-checked and did NOT move. FanDuel added as a new book (-156/+136 trophy; 90-min +130/+190/+270); Kalshi unchanged. Caught and logged a near-miss: a search summary attributed '-136' to BetMGM implying a move to Argentina; the source shows -136 is Kalshi.

Financial thread re-searched: no movement. Rio Times (Jul 9) re-verified the corpus and independently describes TourProdEnter's owner by role without naming him, converging with this site's Run 1 editorial rule. A '$300M' framing and an owner-naming version were NOT adopted (sources 403/404; $300M unverifiable in anything openable; naming breaks the role-not-name rule).

Final squad news chased and mostly NOT adopted: a FanDuel preview claimed Pino 'still out' and Spain 'unbeaten in 36 straight'. ESPN/Sports Mole show Pino's tests were an AC sprain (not a fracture) and he was fit for the France semi - the betting blurb is stale. Streak uncorroborated. No squad edits; recorded so Run 6 doesn't re-chase it.
Hypothesis bands unchanged (H1 2-8, H2 70-90/45-65, H3 35-55, H4 30-50). Nothing since Run 4 touches them; the final is H4's outstanding test.
dataChangesThisRun: bracket.ts unchanged (no match played since Run 4).

models.ts: OPTA retired the stale Jul 12 figures -> ESP .569 / ARG .431 (derived, documented); added publishedPostSF1 {ESP .5615, ENG .2338, ARG .2047, FRA 0} for provenance; url now points at the live bracket article; doc comment carries the full derivation + back-solve. BOOKMAKERS doc comment fixed (it still described France as 'the clear favorite everywhere' - stale) and records the no-move re-check + FanDuel. AUTHOR_RANGES values UNCHANGED; comment rewritten to note four fresh agreeing anchors.
timeline.json unchanged (no real-world event since Run 4).

sections.json: whowins-intro (en+es) - replaced the now-false 'Opta hadn't published a post-semifinal refresh' caveat with the reconciliation, including the plain-language disclosure that the Opta column is a conditioned derivation.
WhoWinsPage.astro: added 'tnt-opta-postsf1' to the models-table SourceRefs so the new claim traces to its source (one-line citation wiring).
openForNextRun: If Opta publishes an explicit post-SF2 pair, replace the derived .569/.431 with the printed figures and drop the conditioning language from models.ts + whowins-intro.
Run-number drift: updates.json tops out at Log run 3 (which narrates research Run 4). Pending recommendation is numbered Log run 4 while research is Run 5. Reconcile deliberately.
The Jul 19 final resolves the champion, H4's regression test, and the Tapia travel-bond return date - all in one weekend.
